Container.Name: SimpleLogin-AIO
Container.Overview:
SimpleLogin is an open-source email alias service. It allows users to create disposable email addresses to protect their real identity from spam and data breaches.
This **All-in-One (AIO)** template is explicitly designed for Unraid beginners. You do **NOT** need to set up any external databases (no separate PostgreSQL or Redis containers required). Everything needed to run SimpleLogin, including the Postfix MTA router and database persistence, is automatically provisioned inside this single container and safely mapped to your appdata folder.
First-Run Steps: 1. Provide your domain name in the App URL and Email Domain fields. 2. Provide your chosen SMTP Relay credentials (so you can send emails out, even if your ISP blocks port 25). 3. Click Apply. 4. The container will take about 30 seconds on first boot to self-generate its internal secure databases and DKIM keys. 5. Read the container logs after it starts to grab your automatically generated DKIM DNS records!
*Advanced Users:* If you want to use external databases, simply fill out the hidden "Advanced: External DB_URI" variable. Otherwise, leave it blank for zero-configuration mode.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: email, alias, proxy, privacy, proton, forwarding, self-contained

Config.Name: Appdata Directory
Config.Description: The master folder where the internal database, Redis queue, and DKIM keys are stored safely on your array. Back this folder up!

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: The port for the Web UI.

Config.Name: SMTP Inbound Port
Config.Description: Public SMTP port. You must forward Port 25 from your router to this Unraid server's IP address so the internet can deliver mail to your aliases.

Config.Name: App URL
Config.Description: The public-facing URL you will use to access the dashboard. Expected format: http(s)://app.domain.com

Config.Name: Email Domain
Config.Description: The primary domain used to create aliases.

Config.Name: Support Email
Config.Description: The address from which transactional system emails (like password resets) are sent.

Config.Name: Flask Secret
Config.Description: A random passcode used to secure browser cookies. Type some random gibberish here.

Config.Name: SMTP Relay Mode
Config.Description: Most ISPs block outbound Port 25, so you can't send mail directly. Select an external relay provider here to route your outgoing alias emails, and fill in the credentials in the Advanced view.
Config.Option: Direct Delivery (Requires open outbound ISP Port 25)

Config.Name: Advanced: External DB_URI
Config.Description: Leave BLANK to use the internal auto-configuring database. Fill this with a postgresql:// connection string to disable the internal DB and route outside the container.

Config.Name: Advanced: External Redis URL
Config.Description: Leave BLANK to use the internal Redis queue. Fill this in to disable the internal queue and route outside.

Config.Name: Disable Registration
Config.Description: Set to 1 to block public registration (recommended).
